comments.body: Seems like IDE didn't import the dependency from Maven. Try re-importing Maven project.
Network issue... reload configuration in IDE ... or clear the cache and delete .idea and reimport ...
Once network connectivity is established, check the local repository. If the download failed, there could be a corrupt file. Delete the artifact folder.
But how does the tests that run in the whole project work (Lifecycle - test)? If there was a problem with the library, then most likely they would not work at all

If you use proxies or custom Maven repositories - make sure you configure them in Maven settings.xml file properly. The repositories IDE uses for downloading the Junit launcher (needed to run the JUnit tests) are located under Settings(Preferences) | Build, Execution, Deployment | Remote Jar Repositories
Do you mean the m2 folder? I have the dependency there but still getting this error in intellij 2023.1

answers.body: The issue was resolved by manually adding JUnit Platform Launcher files to the local repository. This is probably not the optimal solution, but it worked for me

You have to delete a file: path.macro.xml . This macro contains an incorrect maven repository path.

macOS: /Users/[current-user]/Library/Application Support/JetBrains/[current-version]/options/path.macros.xml and restart the IDE.

The solution for me was the hint of Andrey's comment above (checking Intellij > Settings > Build, Execution, Deployment > Remote Jar Repositories: Why does JUnit platform error appear when running a single test? )

According to Intellij Logs (Help > Show Logs In Explorer > idea.log) I always got an HTTP 401 when trying to start a Junit Test and Intellij wants to download the junit-platform-launcher jar.

But when building with Maven (via "mvn") it worked. So the credentials of my settings.xml were fine.

I then found the above comment and also this Intellij documentation section:

"Note that IntelliJ IDEA can load libraries from Maven even if you don't use Maven as a build tool for your project." S: https://www.jetbrains.com/help/idea/library.html#configure-custom-remote-repo

So for my understandsting that means Intellij will download stuff from Maven Central even if you don't necessarily have a Maven project opened/imported. So that would probably also mean Intellij will not look into any settings.xml even if you defined one in the Intellij settings?

So I looked into the Intellij > Settings > Build, Execution, Deployment > Remote Jar Repositories and there was one invalid URL (environment variable not being resolved) and also other URLs of our Artifactory/Nexus which are being used in our Maven settings.xml.

Removing only the invalid URL and letting the valid internal Artifactory/Nexus repository urls there did not help. So with above understanding that Intelli does not look into the given/defined settings.xml (and therefore not reading the internal Artifactory/Nexus repositorys' credentials to get access to the same repository urls defined in the "Remote Jar Repositories" section) it would of course get a HTTP 401 as it can't authenticate. It seems only public accessible / non auth repositories are allowed. Super weird. Maybe I will contact Jetbrains about that.

2 solutions:
-Only use the official public Maven Central URL: Remove all other URLs there / click on "reset defaults" Button.

-Add your Artifactory/Nexus credentials to the URL defined in the "Remote Jar Repositories" (the pattern https://USER:PASSWORD@URL). Then it worked too! But you will have written down your credentials as plain text then..
So why does Intellij not prompt for credentials when using the raw URL?

title: Why does JUnit platform error appear when running a single test?
body:
An error is thrown when running a single test: Failed to resolve org.junit.platform:junit-platform-launcher:1.7.0

The test is launched by clicking on the button in the form of a green triangle next to the name of the method.

But if you run testing of the whole project through maven (lifecycle -> test), then such an error is not visible, and all tests work quietly.

To use JUnit, this dependency is used:

<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
    <scope>test</scope>
</dependency>

The rest of the team who work on this project (one repository, different branches) do not have such problems. What can be causing this error? IDE used - IntelliJ IDEA

UPD: I've tried everything. Deleting the .idea folder had no effect, nor did re-downloading all dependencies. Clearing the cache also had no effect

UPD 2: Proxy is already specified
